  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "For “Clean this up,” which scope do you prefer? A) Sentence-level copy edit only (grammar, concision, tone) with no structural or claim changes; B) Paragraph/section reorganization and flow tightening with minor claim smoothing; or C) Conceptual reframing of the contribution and positioning versus prior work (may alter claims and novelty framing). Please reply with A, B, or C.",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.6": "The phrase “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level editing for clarity, concision, and tone without changing technical content.",
    "RX.1": "The request is ambiguous in scope, so we must surface and document assumptions and seek confirmation.",
    "RX.2": "While polishing, we need to avoid inadvertently strengthening or adding claims beyond what the text supports.",
    "RX.3": "We must adhere strictly to the chosen scope (e.g., copy-edit only unless otherwise specified) and avoid scope creep.",
    "RX.4": "Edits should not misrepresent results, costs, or prior work in ways that could mislead readers.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should be traceable so the author can verify what was modified and ensure fidelity to the original."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: For “Clean this up,” which scope do you prefer? A) Sentence-level copy edit only (grammar, concision, tone) with no structural or claim changes; B) Paragraph/section reorganization and flow tightening with minor claim smoothing; or C) Conceptual reframing of the contribution and positioning versus prior work (may alter claims and novelty framing). Please reply with A, B, or C.

rationale.R1.6: The phrase “Clean this up” most directly implies sentence- and paragraph-level editing for clarity, concision, and tone without changing technical content.
rationale.RX.1: The request is ambiguous in scope, so we must surface and document assumptions and seek confirmation.
rationale.RX.2: While polishing, we need to avoid inadvertently strengthening or adding claims beyond what the text supports.
rationale.RX.3: We must adhere strictly to the chosen scope (e.g., copy-edit only unless otherwise specified) and avoid scope creep.
rationale.RX.4: Edits should not misrepresent results, costs, or prior work in ways that could mislead readers.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should be traceable so the author can verify what was modified and ensure fidelity to the original.
